pr.title: Fix postprocess assert caused by properly ordered code
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Fixed a tooling-side postprocess.py assertion triggered by code whose epilogue instructions appeared in a valid order. The change separates tracking of an `addi r1, r1, 0x18` stack-restore instruction from tracking of `mtlr`, so the postprocessor can record an epilogue ending in `blr` without misclassifying the `addi` as an `mtlr` position.
what_changed: In `impl_postprocess_elf`, added a separate `addi_pos` variable initialized alongside `mtlr_pos`.
When encountering `blr` (`0x4E800020`), the postprocessor now appends an epilogue using `addi_pos` if an `addi r1, r1, 0x18` was seen and no `mtlr` was pending.
After processing a `blr`, both `mtlr_pos` and `addi_pos` are reset.
When encountering `addi r1, r1, 0x18` (`0x38210018`) with no pending `mtlr`, the code now records it in `addi_pos` instead of overloading `mtlr_pos`.
smart_moves: Avoided overloading `mtlr_pos` with a non-`mtlr` instruction position, making the epilogue scanner's state more semantically accurate.
Kept the fix narrowly scoped to the postprocess epilogue-detection logic, with only one changed file and a small diff.
Handled the `addi`-then-`blr` case explicitly rather than weakening or removing the later assertion entirely.
decomp_lessons: Tooling that scans generated assembly should track instruction roles separately; using one variable for multiple instruction kinds can produce false asserts once code ordering improves.
A postprocess assertion failure is not always evidence of bad generated code; it may indicate that the tool's pattern recognizer is too narrow.
When matching epilogues, treat stack restoration instructions like `addi r1, r1, imm` as distinct from link-register restoration instructions like `mtlr`.
naming_conventions: Existing local naming pattern uses instruction-position variables with `_pos` suffix, such as `mtlr_pos`; the fix extends this with `addi_pos`.
Hex instruction constants are documented with nearby comments, e.g. `# addi r1, r1, 0x18` for `0x38210018`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recognizes `addi r1, r1, 0x18` by exact instruction word `0x38210018`.
Epilogue detection records instruction-position pairs, including `(mtlr_pos, blr_pos)` and now the fallback `(addi_pos, blr_pos)` when there is no pending `mtlr`.
State is cleared at `blr` boundaries to avoid carrying epilogue candidates into subsequent instruction scans.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/postprocess.py epilogue detection involving mtlr_pos or addi_pos.
Search for postprocess assertion failures involving lone mtlr or addi r1 r1 0x18.
Review whether other instruction scanners overload position variables for multiple instruction roles.
